title: clerodendrum comans
intro: Clerodendrum comans, also known as the 'Mauve Bells' or 'Slipper Flower', is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Clerodendrum comans are said to include relief from digestive issues, colds, and fever, as well as its use as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
